message:
Improve search tag filtering and simplify field match detection

Two search behavior changes and a performance improvement:

**Tag filter semantics (engine.ts + page.tsx):** Changed tag filtering from
match-ALL to match-ANY. Previously, selecting tags "react" and "typescript"
required a prompt to have BOTH tags — overly restrictive and confusing when
users expect additive filtering. Now a prompt matches if it has ANY of the
selected tags, which is the standard UX for tag-based filtering in content
browsing interfaces. Both the core search engine and the page-level filter
are updated to use .some() instead of .every().

**Field match detection (engine.ts):** Replaced the tokenize-then-Set approach
for detecting which fields matched a query with simple string .includes()
checks on lowercased field content. The previous code tokenized every field
of every result into Sets just to report which fields matched — expensive
work that only affects the "matched in: title, tags" metadata, not ranking.
The BM25 scoring (which does need proper tokenization) is unaffected. The
new approach is O(fields × terms) with simple substring checks instead of
O(fields × tokens) with Set construction and lookups.

**Result limits (page.tsx):** Reduced the search API limit from 50 to 40 and
added a client-side cap of 48 results when no search/category/tag filters
are active, preventing the initial "browse all" view from rendering an
overwhelming number of cards.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
